name: Q727217
abstract: Gene Ramey (April 4, 1913 – December 8, 1984) was an American jazz double bassist. Ramey was born in Austin, Texas, United States, and played trumpet in college, but switched to contrabass when playing with George Corley's Royal Aces, The Moonlight Serenaders, and Terrence Holder.

concerts.label: Jazz Concert with Sarah Vaughan, Erroll Garner, Lester Young and Others
Bel Canto Foundation Jazz Concert to Benefit Musicians

about_quotes.speaker: Mandel Johnny
about_quotes.evidence: When I came in Gene Ramey was there and that was great but Gene wanted to go home, back to Kansas City at the time and we started going through bass players.
about_quotes.doc_title: Mandel Johnny — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Konitz Lee
about_quotes.evidence: The record was with Art Taylor and Gene Ramey and they played beautifully for both of us... I was playing with the rhythm section
about_quotes.doc_title: Konitz Lee — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jay McShann
about_quotes.evidence: Gene had been with her. But at this time, I got Gene, so that worked out good.

about_quotes.speaker: Lou Donaldson
about_quotes.evidence: We had a band with Kenny Dorham and Art Taylor and Gene Ramey.
about_quotes.doc_title: Lou Donaldson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Johnny Varro
about_quotes.evidence: I was on a band once where I was the only white kid in the band. There was Jonah Jones on trumpet, Coleman Hawkins on tenor, Benny Green on trombone, Jo Jones on drums, various bass players, Gene Ramey maybe on bass and myself
about_quotes.doc_title: Johnny Varro interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clearwater Beach, Florida, March 16, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Bud Freeman
about_quotes.evidence: I remember running into him at one of those jazz festivals in Colorado and I told him that story about Lester and he just fell out laughing and then he told me a beautiful story about Lester.
